name: Oliver Lake
abstract: Oliver Lake (born September 14, 1942) is an American jazz saxophonist, flutist, composer, poet, and visual artist. He is known mainly for alto saxophone, but he also performs on soprano and flute.

concerts.label: Synthesis: The Music of Reggie Workman
JVC Jazz Festival: Steve Reich and Musicians with World Saxophone Quartet

about_quotes.speaker: Reggie Watkins
about_quotes.evidence: I had the incredible opportunity to play and back up Oliver Lake.
about_quotes.doc_title: Reggie Watkins interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, May 22, 2018

about_quotes.speaker: Jackie McLean
about_quotes.evidence: I like them for their honesty and that they do what they do. I like Oliver especially, because Oliver will tell you in a minute that he tried to play like me.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jackie McLean — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Arturo O'Farrill
about_quotes.evidence: I embraced Oliver Lake, I embraced Rashied Ali, I embraced — so I had a little group I think in 1976 that I was playing with, of musicians, Pablo Calogero...
about_quotes.doc_title: Arturo O'Farrill interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 6, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Don Alias
about_quotes.evidence: I was doing an interview in Japan with a young drummer by the name of Gene Lake. Did you hear that name? Oliver Lake’s son.
about_quotes.doc_title: Don Alias interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 6, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Longineu Parsons
about_quotes.evidence: thinking about people like Oliver Lake and the people I played with
about_quotes.doc_title: Longineu Parsons interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 17, 2026
